_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Glaucoma Surgery (Trabeculectomy only) with or without Mitomycin C, including postoperative medications for 12 weeks (and wherever surgical or laser procedures required for bleb augmentation and anterior chamber maintenance)
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Moderate/advanced open angle glaucoma with optic nerve damage and visual field loss, requiring IOP reduction of ≥25-50%, where trabeculectomy ± Mitomycin C is a treatment consideration.
Acute or chronic closed angle glaucoma (pupil block) requiring surgical intervention (e.g., lens extraction/IOL + trabeculectomy + Mitomycin C).
Closed angle glaucoma (plateau iris) requiring surgical intervention (e.g., lens extraction/IOL + trabeculectomy + Mitomycin C).
Diagnosis backed by clinical signs, symptoms, and ophthalmic examination, and failure to respond to conservative medical therapy.

preauth.label: Indication (Open angle Glaucoma: Moderate/Advanced Glaucoma; Closed angle Glaucoma)
Documentation of recent field of vision (Perimetry examination)

claim.label: Discharge summary with Intraocular pressure, Fundus and corneal diameter
Intraoperative photograph with patient ID, time and date (optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Raised Intra-ocular pressure not controlled by medication?
Are Detailed clinical notes available (indicating condition of optic nerve head and intra-ocular pressure, clinical symptoms and visual acuity)?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and was the surgery successful?
Is the intraocular pressure (IOP) on first post op day and IOP on day of discharge mentioned?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Was the intra operative photograph submitted?

common_queries: Optic nerve damage not documented or not present
Intra-ocular pressure not documented or documented below 24mmHg threshold without justification
Intra-ocular pressure documented as controlled by medication
Clinical notes missing or lacking detail on optic nerve head, IOP, symptoms, or visual acuity
Perimetry examination documentation missing or incomplete
Clinical photograph missing or not clearly labelled
Discharge summary missing or lacking IOP, Fundus, or corneal diameter details
Procedure/operative notes missing or not detailing surgical steps
Intraoperative photograph not submitted (if applicable, optional)

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Glaucoma Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
Glaucoma is one of the leading causes of Blindness after Cataract. Glaucoma refers to a group of diseases in which optic nerve damage is the primary pathology leading to vision loss.
The most common forms of Glaucoma are- Open angle and Closed angle Glaucoma.
In East Asia vision loss from Closed angle Glaucoma is more common, in western world Open angle Glaucoma is more common.
Risk factor: Raised Intra-ocular pressure (IOP) is a major risk factor for loss of sight from open and closed angle Glaucoma. Raised IOP leads to damage of optic nerve
referral_criteria: Acute primary angle closure with uncontrolled IOP on maximum therapy and facilities for laser peripheral iridotomy are not available
Facilities for cataract surgery not available in lens induced glaucomas (phacomorphic)
Children (acute intraocular pressure lowering measures initiated prior to referral; laser or surgical therapy to be done at higher centres)
Secondary glaucomas without facilities to treat the underlying cause (for examplelaser iridotomy for pupil block/ surgery (ex. in microspherophakia or lensectomyfor subluxated crystalline lens)
Previous failed trabeculectomy uncontrolled on maximum antiglaucoma therapy
Post congenital cataract surgery with uncontrolled IOP on therapy
Complications of filtration surgery requiring surgical intervention, Failing bleb, Large choroidal haemorrhage/ effusions, Bleb leaks, Blebitis and endophthalmitis
